summary: postinstall exfiltrates installer secrets and env to hardcoded bare-IP C2
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a gated check (hostname matches a 12-char hex Docker container ID, cwd contains /app/node_modules, registry is not a CN mirror) and then runs a shell payload that reads /root/.npmrc, /run/secrets, /var/run/secrets, walks /proc/[0-9]*/environ to capture other processes' environment variables, and grep-scrapes /root /opt /usr/local/bin /run /var/run /tmp for AWS/TOKEN/SECRET/FLAG patterns. The collected output is base64-encoded into a YAML 'manifest' and PUT over plain HTTP to http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-839201. The execution gate (developer/scanner machines see no activity, only container-shaped target hosts trigger the payload) is an evasion mechanism, not a legitimate diagnostic feature. The package is a credential harvester / exfil dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
